| :---- | :---- |
| **Email Address/es**  | [yhasmennogales04@gmail.com](mailto:yhasmennogales04@gmail.com) [carylatienzaa@gmail.com](mailto:carylatienzaa@gmail.com) |
| **Affiliation/s** | Polytechnic University of the Philippines |
| **Declaration of eligibility**  | *I/we hereby declare that the submitted entry complies with all eligibility requirements: the participant(s) are **aged between 18 to 30 years old**; are based in the Philippines; and are undergraduate or graduate students, early researchers, entrepreneurs, or members of a start-up. The entry presents an innovative idea or project that contributes to economic development and societal improvement. For team submissions, the group does not exceed the maximum of three (3) members.*  |
|  **Innovation Industry Clusters *(select only one)***  |                               Life Sciences and Digital Health Cluster                                     Manufacturing and Construction                                   **IT-BPM**  |
| **Title**  | ReadiLY: AI-Powered Early Talent Intelligence System |

| I. Executive Summary *(max 500 words)* |
| ----- |
| ReadiLY is an AI-powered early talent intelligence system that strengthens foundational literacy as a pathway to workforce readiness in the digital economy. Across Southeast Asia, many students experience “learning poverty,” limiting their ability to read, comprehend, and process information effectively. These foundational gaps directly affect long-term workforce readiness, particularly in sectors such as IT-BPM that require strong communication, comprehension, and cognitive skills. ReadiLY addresses this issue by transforming traditional reading assessments into real-time, AI-driven diagnostic systems. Using a teacher’s smartphone, students read aloud while the system analyzes fluency, accuracy, and error patterns. Within seconds, it generates a diagnostic profile, identifies specific learning gaps, and flags students at risk of falling behind. Unlike conventional tools that provide delayed and surface-level results, ReadiLY enables immediate, data-driven intervention. This shifts assessment from a passive activity into an actionable intelligence system. From an industry perspective, ReadiLY contributes to human capital development by strengthening core skills at an early stage. By improving literacy foundations, it supports the development of a more capable and adaptable future workforce, reducing the need for costly remediation later. The system is designed to be offline-first, mobile-based, and scalable across ASEAN contexts, making it practical for deployment in resource-constrained environments. ReadiLY positions early education as a strategic entry point for long-term economic growth, bridging the gap between foundational learning and workforce readiness. |
| **II. Background / Rationale** *(max 500 words)*  |
| Foundational literacy is a critical determinant of both educational success and long-term workforce capability. However, persistent gaps in early reading proficiency continue to affect learning outcomes and human capital development in the Philippines, creating downstream challenges for industry. According to the **World Bank (2022)**, a significant proportion of children in low- and middle-income countries experience “learning poverty,” defined as the inability to read and understand a simple text by age 10\. In the Philippine context, national and international assessments such as **PISA (OECD, 2019\)** consistently show that many learners struggle with reading comprehension, limiting their ability to engage effectively with academic content and, eventually, workplace tasks. Despite the importance of early literacy, current assessment practices remain limited in both efficiency and diagnostic depth. In many public school settings, teachers manage large class sizes and rely on manual oral reading assessments, such as those outlined in **DepEd early grade literacy frameworks**. These require evaluating students individually and recording results through paper-based or spreadsheet systems, making the process time-intensive and difficult to scale. This creates a **systemic visibility gap**, where teachers are unable to accurately identify which students are struggling and, more importantly, why. As a result, three persistent challenges emerge. First, learning gaps are often identified too late, as assessments are not conducted frequently or comprehensively. Second, existing methods provide limited diagnostic insight, focusing on scores rather than underlying causes of difficulty. Third, intervention strategies are often generalized, as teachers lack the data needed to tailor instruction effectively. Over time, these gaps accumulate and directly affect core competencies such as comprehension, communication, and information processing. According to the **UNESCO Global Education Monitoring Report (2021)**, foundational learning deficits significantly reduce students’ ability to participate effectively in higher education and the workforce. From an industry perspective, this issue extends beyond education and into economic performance. The **IT-BPM sector**, a key driver of growth in the Philippines, increasingly requires workers who can interpret complex information, communicate clearly, and operate within digital systems. Persistent weaknesses in foundational literacy contribute to **skills gaps in communication and cognitive processing**, which in turn affect operational efficiency, customer-facing performance, and training costs within organizations. Current workforce development strategies focus on reskilling and upskilling programs. While necessary, these interventions often address deficits that originate much earlier in the learning pipeline. This creates a structural inefficiency, where organizations invest heavily in remediation instead of benefiting from a workforce that is already prepared for knowledge-based roles. Therefore, there is a clear need for a scalable system that enables early, accurate, and actionable literacy assessment. By identifying learning gaps at their earliest stage, such a system can support timely intervention, improve learning outcomes, and strengthen long-term workforce quality. ReadiLY is designed to address this gap by integrating AI-driven diagnostic capabilities into everyday classroom practice, enabling a more proactive, data-driven, and efficient approach to foundational skill development. |
| **III. Discussion of Project Proposal/ Innovative Idea** *(max. 2000 words)* |
|  ReadiLY is an AI-powered literacy intelligence system that transforms traditional reading assessment into a real-time, data-driven workflow:  **Read → Analyze → Diagnose → Act → Improve.**  Rather than introducing a new activity, the system enhances an existing classroom process—oral reading—by embedding AI-driven analysis and decision support directly into it. **System Workflow** The system operates through a simple, teacher-led process designed for real classroom conditions. A teacher initiates a reading session using a smartphone and selects a student profile. The student reads a short passage aloud while the application captures audio input in real time. ReadiLY processes this input using automatic speech recognition and machine learning models. It evaluates multiple dimensions of reading performance, including: Reading speed (words per minute) Pronunciation accuracy Pauses and hesitations Word-level errors such as substitutions, omissions, and repetitions Instead of producing a single score, the system performs **pattern-based analysis**. For example, repeated substitutions may indicate decoding or phonics-related difficulties, while frequent pauses may signal fluency challenges. These patterns are mapped to specific literacy competencies, allowing the system to generate a structured diagnostic profile for each learner. Within seconds, the teacher receives: A performance summary Clearly identified learning gaps Early indicators of learning risk This enables immediate, targeted intervention, eliminating delays between assessment and action. **Core Capabilities** ReadiLY is intentionally designed around five core capabilities to ensure focus and usability: **AI-powered oral reading assessment** for real-time data capture **Learning gap diagnosis** to identify specific literacy weaknesses **Error pattern detection** for deeper behavioral analysis **Predictive early warning system** to flag at-risk learners **Teacher dashboard** for actionable insights Together, these capabilities transform assessment from a passive measurement task into an active decision-support system. **Technical Architecture** ReadiLY uses a lightweight, mobile-first architecture optimized for accessibility, scalability, and real-time performance. At its core, the system integrates: **Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR)** to convert spoken input into text **Fluency and error analysis models** to evaluate reading behavior **Classification models** to map patterns to literacy competencies **Predictive models** to identify early indicators of learning risk Processing is performed primarily on-device using optimized machine learning models, enabling real-time feedback without reliance on continuous internet connectivity. When a connection is available, data is securely synchronized with a cloud database for storage and analytics. This hybrid architecture ensures both **low latency and scalability**, making the system suitable for deployment in real-world classroom environments. **Technical Feasibility Diagram** The Figure 1 under the references, illustrates the end-to-end technical workflow of ReadiLY, highlighting its on-device AI processing and offline first design.  The system begins with student voice input captured through a mobile device during a reading activity. This input is processed locally using speech recognition models to convert audio into text, followed by fluency and error analysis to evaluate reading behavior. The processed data is then passed through classification and predictive models, which generate structured diagnostic outputs, including learning gaps and early risk indicators. These outputs are presented through a teacher-facing dashboard, enabling immediate instructional decisions. A key innovation of the architecture is its edge AI capability, where core processing occurs directly on the device. This ensures zero-latency feedback, reduces dependency on internet connectivity, and enhances usability in resource-constrained environments. Cloud synchronization is optional and used primarily for data aggregation, long-term tracking, and analytics, ensuring both operational flexibility and scalability.  **Technical Validation and Supporting Technologies** The feasibility of ReadiLY’s architecture is supported by established advancements in artificial intelligence and mobile deployment: Modern speech recognition models such as **Whisper (Radford et al., 2022\)** and **wav2vec 2.0 (Baevski et al., 2020\)** demonstrate high accuracy in converting speech to text, even in diverse linguistic contexts. Lightweight deployment frameworks such as **TensorFlow Lite (Google, 2023\)** enable efficient on-device machine learning inference, making real-time processing feasible on standard mobile devices. Offline-first system architectures have been widely adopted in low-connectivity environments to ensure reliability and scalability (**Kumar et al., 2019**). These technologies collectively support the practicality of implementing ReadiLY within existing infrastructure constraints. **Integration into Existing Systems**  ReadiLY is designed to integrate seamlessly into current classroom workflows. Teachers are not required to adopt new teaching methods or undergo extensive training. Instead, the system enhances existing reading activities by providing immediate diagnostic insights. This approach minimizes adoption barriers, reduces resistance, and supports rapid deployment in public education systems. **Data and Continuous Feedback Loop**  ReadiLY operates as a continuous feedback system. Each reading session contributes to a growing dataset of student performance, enabling: Tracking of individual progress over time Identification of class-level learning trends Improvement of diagnostic accuracy This creates a dynamic learning environment where both teachers and students benefit from continuous, data-driven improvement. **Industry and Workforce Relevance** ReadiLY is positioned as an upstream solution to workforce development challenges. In the IT-BPM sector, employees must interpret complex information, communicate effectively, and operate within digital systems. These capabilities are rooted in strong foundational literacy. However, many workforce challenges such as communication gaps, comprehension difficulties, and high training costs—can be traced back to weaknesses in early learning. By enabling early detection and intervention, ReadiLY addresses these challenges at their source. It supports the development of individuals who are better prepared for knowledge-based roles, reducing the need for costly remediation later in the workforce pipeline. In this way, ReadiLY complements existing reskilling and upskilling initiatives by improving the **quality of incoming talent**, strengthening long-term industry performance. **Strategic Positioning** ReadiLY is not positioned as a standalone education tool, but as a **foundational layer in human capital development**. It bridges early education and workforce outcomes by: Improving the speed and accuracy of literacy assessment Enabling data-driven instructional decisions Strengthening competencies essential for industry This positions ReadiLY as a scalable, technology-driven solution that addresses both educational and economic priorities. |
| **IV. A: Potential Impact** *(max. 500 words)* Deliver potential measurable business value for the cluster’s economic growth |
| ReadiLY delivers measurable economic value by strengthening workforce readiness at its earliest stage, shifting talent development from reactive remediation to proactive prevention. In knowledge-based industries such as IT-BPM, employee performance is highly dependent on comprehension, communication, and the ability to process information accurately. Weaknesses in these foundational skills result in increased onboarding time, higher training costs, and lower productivity in both operational and customer-facing roles. Recent workforce studies highlight that foundational cognitive and communication skills remain a major gap affecting employability and performance in digital economies (**World Economic Forum, 2023; McKinsey & Company, 2021**). ReadiLY addresses this challenge at its source. By enabling early detection and targeted intervention in literacy development, the system reduces the likelihood that these gaps persist into the workforce. From a business perspective, this creates direct and measurable value: **Reduced training costs:** Even a modest 10–15% reduction in foundational skills remediation can significantly lower onboarding expenses in large-scale IT-BPM operations, where training is a major cost driver (**McKinsey & Company, 2021**) **Improved productivity:** Employees with stronger comprehension skills can process instructions faster, reduce errors, and perform tasks more efficiently, contributing to higher overall productivity (**OECD, 2021**) **Enhanced service quality:** In customer-facing environments such as call centers, improved communication leads to clearer interactions, higher customer satisfaction, and better performance metrics (**World Economic Forum, 2023**) **Faster onboarding and deployment:** Workforce entrants require less time to reach operational readiness, improving operational efficiency and reducing time-to-productivity (**LinkedIn Economic Graph, 2022**) In high-volume environments where thousands of employees are trained annually, even incremental improvements in literacy-related competencies can translate into substantial cost savings and performance gains. Beyond immediate business outcomes, ReadiLY generates structured, large-scale data on literacy development. These insights can support more effective workforce planning by identifying emerging skill gaps early, enabling organizations and policymakers to align training investments with actual needs (**UNESCO, 2022**). A key differentiator of ReadiLY is its ability to **shift workforce investment from remediation to prevention**. Most workforce interventions occur after inefficiencies have already materialized, requiring significant resources to correct. ReadiLY addresses these inefficiencies at their origin, reducing the need for costly downstream interventions. At scale, this results in: A higher-quality and more adaptable talent pipeline Reduced operational inefficiencies across industries Stronger alignment between education outcomes and labor market demands By strengthening foundational competencies before individuals enter the workforce, ReadiLY contributes to long-term improvements in productivity, competitiveness, and economic growth. Ultimately, ReadiLY positions early literacy not only as an educational priority, but as a **strategic economic lever for workforce development and industry performance**. |
| **IV. B: Novelty and Innovation** *(max 500 words)* The extent to which the idea or solution is new and innovative, as opposed to incremental improvements on existing solutions |
| ReadiLY introduces a fundamental shift in literacy assessment by transforming it from a static measurement process into a real-time, AI-driven intelligence system. Existing solutions in the literacy space fall into two primary categories. Assessment tools such as DIBELS and STAR Reading are designed to evaluate performance after reading activities, typically producing standardized scores with limited diagnostic depth. While effective for benchmarking, these tools provide delayed feedback and do not explain the underlying causes of reading difficulties. On the other hand, digital learning platforms such as Raz-Kids and Duolingo focus on content delivery and engagement. These platforms support practice and exposure but do not offer real-time behavioral analysis or predictive insights into learning gaps. As a result, current solutions either **measure outcomes** or **deliver content**, but do not function as continuous intelligence systems. ReadiLY introduces a third category: a **literacy intelligence system** that integrates assessment, diagnosis, and prediction into a single real-time workflow. The first key innovation is **real-time AI-driven analysis**. By leveraging on-device speech recognition and machine learning models, ReadiLY evaluates reading behavior as it occurs, eliminating the delay between assessment and feedback. Advances in speech AI have demonstrated the feasibility of accurate, real-time processing across diverse contexts (**Radford et al., 2022; Baevski et al., 2020**), which ReadiLY applies directly within classroom environments. The second innovation is **pattern-based diagnostic intelligence**. Rather than relying on aggregate scores, the system analyzes recurring reading behaviors—such as substitutions, omissions, and hesitations—and maps them to specific literacy competencies. This enables precise identification of the root causes of learning difficulty, aligning with emerging approaches in learning analytics that emphasize behavior-driven insights (**OECD, 2021**). The third innovation is the integration of a **predictive early warning system**. By identifying patterns associated with future learning risk, ReadiLY enables proactive intervention before gaps become severe. Predictive analytics has been widely recognized as a key driver of early intervention in education and workforce systems (**World Economic Forum, 2023**), yet remains largely absent in foundational literacy tools. These capabilities are unified through an **offline-first, edge AI architecture**, where core processing occurs directly on the device. This ensures real-time performance, reduces infrastructure dependency, and enables deployment in low-resource environments. Edge AI has been identified as a critical enabler for scalable and cost-efficient AI systems (**McKinsey & Company, 2021**). Unlike existing tools that operate in isolation—either assessing or teaching—ReadiLY functions as a continuous intelligence layer within the learning process. It does not simply evaluate reading; it interprets behavior, predicts risk, and informs action in real time. **ReadiLY is the first system to transform everyday reading into a continuous stream of actionable intelligence, directly linking assessment to intervention.** By bridging the gap between evaluation and action, ReadiLY enables more precise teaching, faster learning progress, and stronger foundations for long-term workforce development. |
| **IV. C: Feasibility** *(max 500 words)* Whether the idea or solution can be realistically developed and implemented within a reasonable timeframe and budget |
| **Implementation Approach** ReadiLY will be developed by adapting pre-trained speech recognition and machine learning models to the context of early-grade reading. Pre-trained models such as *Whisper* (Radford et al., 2022\) and *wav2vec 2.0* (Baevski et al., 2020\) will be **fine-tuned using child speech datasets and reading-specific samples** to improve accuracy in pronunciation, pacing, and common reading errors. Initial training data can be sourced from publicly available child speech datasets and further enhanced through pilot classroom recordings. Model optimization techniques, including quantization and pruning, will be applied to enable efficient on-device inference using frameworks such as *TensorFlow Lite* (Google, 2023). This ensures real-time processing on standard smartphones without requiring high-end hardware. Development will follow a staged approach: **Phase 1:** Model adaptation and prototype development **Phase 2:** Pilot testing and localized data collection **Phase 3:** Iterative improvement and scaled deployment This phased implementation reduces technical uncertainty while enabling continuous validation in real-world settings. **Operational Feasibility** ReadiLY is designed to integrate seamlessly into existing classroom practices. Teachers already conduct oral reading assessments; the system enhances this workflow without requiring new teaching methods or significant behavioral change. A typical session involves a student reading aloud while the teacher uses a smartphone to capture and receive results in real time. To support adoption, the system aligns with existing assessment frameworks used in schools, ensuring familiarity and ease of use. A short onboarding session (approximately 1–2 hours) is sufficient for teachers to begin using the system effectively. A pilot implementation can be conducted with: **1–3 schools 2–3 teachers 100–150 students 2–4 weeks testing period** This allows validation under real classroom conditions before broader rollout. **Cost Feasibility** ReadiLY is designed as a cost-efficient solution for large-scale deployment. **No additional hardware required:** Utilizes existing teacher smartphones **Minimal infrastructure dependency:** Core processing is performed on-device **Reduced development cost:** Built on open-source AI models Compared to traditional digital learning systems that require dedicated devices, licensing fees, or continuous connectivity, ReadiLY significantly lowers the cost barrier through **software-based deployment and edge processing**. This makes the system financially viable for public education systems and scalable without major capital investment. **Risk Assessment and Mitigation** Potential implementation risks have been identified and addressed: **Variability in student pronunciation and accents:** Mitigated through fine-tuning on localized and age-specific datasets **Language and dialect diversity:** Addressed through continuous model adaptation and region-specific training data **Background noise in classroom environments:** Managed using noise-robust preprocessing and filtering techniques **Teacher adoption and usability concerns:** Reduced through intuitive design and alignment with existing workflows **Data privacy considerations:** Managed through on-device processing, minimal data collection, and transparent outputs By proactively addressing these challenges, ReadiLY ensures reliable performance in diverse and real-world environments. **Scalability and Deployment** ReadiLY is inherently scalable due to its software-based architecture. Once validated, the system can be distributed through standard mobile application channels without requiring additional infrastructure. Because processing occurs on-device, scaling does not significantly increase operational costs. Localization for different languages and contexts can be achieved through model fine-tuning, enabling expansion without redesigning the system. As a result, scaling ReadiLY depends on **efficient software distribution rather than infrastructure expansion**, making growth both practical and sustainable. **Feasibility Summary ReadiLY is not only feasible to build—it is deployable, scalable and built to change the gap.** |
| **V. References**  |
| Department of Education \[DepEd\] Philippines. *Early Grade Literacy Reports. [https://www.deped.gov.ph/2026/01/16/reading-intervention-ng-deped-naghatid-ng-positibong-resulta-sa-early-grade-learners/](https://www.deped.gov.ph/2026/01/16/reading-intervention-ng-deped-naghatid-ng-positibong-resulta-sa-early-grade-learners/)* LinkedIn (2022). *Global Talent Trends / Economic Graph Report.* [https://economicgraph.linkedin.com/](https://economicgraph.linkedin.com/) McKinsey Global Institute.
